symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetrical shoulder and pelvic girdle pain and stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral proximal girdle involvement is the defining distribution of PMR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound morning stiffness lasting most of the day (>45-60 minutes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged inflammatory morning stiffness is a core diagnostic criterion for PMR.
Degenerative stiffness is typically brief (<30 minutes) and activity-related, not prolonged inflammatory morning stiffness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sparing of the small joints of the hands and feet (proximal-only involvement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PMR characteristically spares the small peripheral joints, involving proximal girdles.
RA typically involves the small joints of the hands/wrists with synovitis; their sparing argues against RA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of cranial GCA features (no headache, scalp tenderness, jaw claudication or visual disturbance)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Screening cranial GCA features negative lowers the immediate probability of GCA but does not remove the need for ongoing safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of distal neurological deficit (normal limb power/sensation, no radicular pain, no sphincter disturbance)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No distal weakness, numbness, radicular pain or sphincter disturbance argues strongly against a root/cord cause of the immobility.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid onset over ~48 hours with profound constitutional fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PMR can present or flare abruptly with prominent constitutional features.
PMR essentially does not occur under 50 and rises steeply with age — a strong pre-test probability modifier.
GCA shares the same older-adult demographic and overlaps with PMR.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated acute-phase markers consistent with an active inflammatory process — supportive of PMR but not specific
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis remains unsupported and steroid response cannot be contextualised; a normal marker set would prompt reconsideration.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised ESR/CRP is expected in active PMR and supports the clinical picture; sealed as it requires pathology not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-tender, pulsatile temporal arteries on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal temporal artery examination lowers GCA probability but cannot exclude it; sealed clinician finding not obtainable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Restricted, painful active range of motion of shoulders/hips with proximal muscle tenderness and preserved distal joints

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painful restricted proximal ROM with girdle tenderness and spared distal joints fits PMR; sealed clinician examination finding.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral proximal girdle pain + prolonged morning stiffness + small-joint sparing in an older adult together form a high-probability PMR constellation that no single feature achieves alone.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acute-phase markers support the PMR diagnosis and provide a baseline to monitor steroid response; arrange via GP/pathology urgently (source ordered these for the next day).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea/electrolytes, LFTs, glucose/HbA1c
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline before commencing glucocorticoid (steroid effects on glucose, screening for alternative causes).
Normal CK helps exclude an inflammatory myopathy; TSH excludes hypothyroid myopathy as a mimic of proximal weakness/stiffness.
Not indicated with a negative distal neurological screen and a bilateral symmetrical proximal inflammatory picture — over-investigation and unnecessary radiation/cost.
